title: ferrotorch-xpu: add pub(crate) fn XpuDevice::new_for_testing(ordinal) so stub-mode op error contract can be verified without hardware (Phase 4 follow-up)
description:
Follow-up from Audit-Fix Phase 4 (#1075). The conformance suite at ferrotorch-xpu/tests/conformance_xpu.rs cannot verify the stub-mode runtime contract for op functions or for make_xpu_tensor without an XpuDevice handle. Today this requires constructing one through XpuDevice::new(0), which is impossible on hardware-less boxes (WSL2: panics on missing Vulkan adapter; no-feature build: returns DeviceUnavailable before allocation).

Add a test-only constructor that bypasses the wgpu adapter probe so stub-mode runtime behaviour can be asserted directly:

  pub(crate) fn new_for_testing(ordinal: usize) -> XpuDevice

Without wgpu: trivially constructs the struct (ordinal-only).
With wgpu: gated #[cfg(test)] or feature='_internal_test'; constructs a minimal handle solely so test harnesses can call op functions and observe the error contract.

Once available, the following cascade_skip points in conformance_xpu.rs become real assertions:

- make_xpu_tensor_errors_without_backend (Phase 4 #1053): assert make_xpu_tensor(vec![1.0], &[1], &dev) returns Err(DeviceUnavailable) on stub builds.
- xpu_device_metadata_parity (Phase 4 #1055): assert format!("{}", dev) == "xpu:0" exercising XpuDevice's Display impl directly (currently only Device::Xpu(0)'s Display is exercised).
- The 23 binary/unary/polynomial op symbols can be invoked with stub args to verify they all return Err(DeviceUnavailable) at runtime.

Audit-fix series is test-only; this src/ change is filed as the unblocking follow-up. Cited from the strengthened Phase 4 tests' cascade_skip messages.
